topic.what: Fix insecure code generation in LLMs using per-CWE activation steering vectors at identified suppression layers
topic.how: Mechanistic diagnosis reveals security knowledge encoded early but computationally inert until final layer; per-vulnerability steering vectors at emergence site (Layer 31) recover secure behavior
topic.why_matters: AI-generated code increasingly enters production; developers exhibit automation bias accepting insecure suggestions; existing mitigations (fine-tuning, prompt engineering, constrained decoding) are insufficient

characteristics.what_it_measures: Secure generation rate improvement on adversarial prompts
characteristics.evidence: LOBO cross-validation, 105 prompt pairs per CWE

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of code generations using secure APIs
characteristics.evidence: End-to-end pipeline evaluation on neutral prompts

characteristics.vs_baseline: Naive impl shows +102% but artifact of longer outputs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Steering gains across different model architectures
characteristics.context: CWE-787 steering effective across Llama, Mistral, Qwen families

characteristics.what_itasures: Early-layer probe accuracy for vulnerability type classification
characteristics.vs_baseline: Adversarial-trained probes fail (66.7%) on neutral prompts
characteristics.evidence: L16 probe, neutral prompt LOBO evaluation
characteristics.context: Vulnerability-type info stable at L16, security decision crystallizes at L31

characteristics.what_it_measures: Layer where security representations become output-relevant
characteristics.vs_baseline: Probes detect from Layer 0 but logit lens shows no emergence until final layers
characteristics.evidence: Logit lens and tuned lens across 5 models
characteristics.context: Pattern holds across all 5 models; Llama shows sudden (250× jump), Mistral shows distributed

characteristics.what_itasures: Diagonal vs off-diagonal performance in transfer matrix
characteristics.vs_baseline: Unified vector retains only 24-40% of per-CWE effectiveness
characteristics.context: Per-CWE vectors are distinct; unified alternatives lose most effectiveness

characteristics.what_it_measures: Model's ability to identify vulnerabilities in code review
characteristics.vs_baseline: Same models produce insecure code in same scenarios
characteristics.evidence: Llama-3.1-8B flags sprintf as dangerous in 90% of review trials but generates it in 93.3% of generation trials
characteristics.context: Format-Reliability Gap: knowledge present but suppressed during generation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Increase in syntax/compilation errors from steering
characteristics.vs_baseline: Steering trades some correctness for security
characteristics.evidence: End-to-end evaluation on neutral vs adversarial prompts
characteristics.context: Acceptable on neutral, concentrated on adversarial distribution

good_for: API-substitution vulnerabilities with clear secure/insecure pairs (CWE-787, CWE-89)
High-security code generation pipelines requiring minimal latency overhead
Scenarios where latent security knowledge exists but is suppressed by format pressure
not_for: Vulnerability types with weak latent knowledge (CWE-134: +4.8 pp only)
Heterogeneous CWEs requiring sub-CWE decomposition (CWE-119 strcpy vs gets)
Real-time high-throughput scenarios where any overhead is critical
requires: Access to model activations at identified intervention layer
Probe training data (prompt pairs per vulnerability type)
Leave-one-out cross-validation for steering vector construction
Early-layer probe for vulnerability type classification before routing
